pattern: Sweep the sorted arrays once, keeping the running min and max seen so far, and at each new array combine its extreme ends with the opposite running extreme from a different array.
hints: Each array is already sorted, so its only useful values are its first and last element.
You must take the two picks from different arrays, so you cannot pair an array's min with its own max.
Process arrays in order; before folding array i in, compare its first/last against the min and max accumulated from arrays before it.

edge_inputs.description: three arrays, max distance spans first and second
two singleton arrays with equal value
extremes inside the same array must not pair
largest spread requires crossing non-adjacent arrays
many arrays each with one element

stress.description: many sorted arrays of small length with values across the full range
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    m = max(2, min(size, 2000))
    arrays = []
    for _ in range(m):
        k = rng.randint(1, 5)
        vals = sorted(rng.randint(-10000, 10000) for _ in range(k))
        arrays.append(vals)
    return [arrays]
